channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 155 replies — 620.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 155 • Updated: 2026-06-01 05:17 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287630
>>62280441
You're thinking the wrong way

62287632
>>62282426
Well, depends on them

62287634
BTC has just tracked the nasdaq for years until recently and now it just sucks ass no matter what happens.

Hype has peaked. Most of it is owned by boomers now and its not a hedge against anything it just dumps the hardest on bad news until boomers, banks and third worlders get tired of bagholding and it goes sub 20k forever

62287690
>>62287634
So you're saying btc is only a big hell hype?

62288099
>>62287634
nonsense

62288112
>>62273994
So it's not worth it?

62288281
>>62271712
Indifference. I got into the market when the BTC was $3K. but I bought Link instead. Pleased with my decision. I respect what it has done and continues to do with the world but I get excited for smart contracts more, which the BTC doesn't have.

62288653
>>62271712
Because I got caught with my pants down avgcost 95k, and I've been trimming it and using that money as play dump money. So far the 15k I sold from Btc and put into Sandisk have turned in 110k already, and the 10k into gold are at 14k, and it's an actual asset. Never buying that retarded shit again. I still have like 30k and maybe I'll keep that, but chances are I'll probably sell and ride the SpaceX pump and dumo with it. Put in 50k into it, sell 50k when it doubles in price, keep the rest as gains. Did the same shit with Micron, Sandisk and silver, take profits and just keep the rest. This is a hype based market and btc has no hype anymore.

62290061
>>62288112
We'll find out when TBVs actually launch.
Then you can judge the product based on what it does, not on what random people online tell you to think.

62290076
>>62275451
Ok, just buy altcoins

62290078
>>62272002
ahahahahahahahah

62290085
>>62275501
If BTC goes to zero, alts are probably even more cooked.

62290480
>>62287464
If it's such an obvious scam, why are you still here watching it?

62290856
>>62271933
There's truth to this, but I dunno about 40k. Buy "dips" now I say

62290931
>>62272803
>>62272874

The conundrum that niggers on here don't understand is that everyone wants upside volatility, but it's the volatility itself that ultimately creates the ceiling.

It's the cute naive chick that ceases to be so once you fuck her. You can't have your cute and fuck it too.

62291823
>>62290931
You're also another nigger

62292614
>>62287393
So how much are you making?

62292863
>>62271712
the absolute best case scenario for bitcoin would be a 3x. there are other coins currently on the market that can and probably will give me a higher roi.

62292885
>>62271712
Someday a scalable bitcoin will fork away from BTC with enough ideological support to effectively upgrade what everyone will agree to be bitcoin. Then I will buy bitcoin. Until then it's pointless to buy a coin just to get the real bitcoin eventually as a dividend fork. Just wait and buy scalable bitcoin when it exists.

62292989
>>62271712
>saylor holding 5% (more due to lost coins)
>trump stink
>american hegemony stink
>quantum fud
>couldnt even act as a hedge during a war which is its only job
>2028 gary gensler revenge tour
Wasn't the plan this whole time to dump on the institutions? The OGs dumped billions and billions of market clips on them and left forever. That was the entire goal and it was accomplished, genuinely wtf more do you want? The win-con was fulfilled, if you didn't make it then come up with a new one or find another asset. Or you can continue DCAing for the next 30 years I guess

62292997
What are you getting, a 2-4x depending on where the bottom is?

62294119
>>62292863
And what is it? Low cap alts?

62294591
>>62271960
They will say the same thing when it hit 200K Kek. Honestly BTC has a lot of potential most especially since only 1% is in Defi, Imagine how huge it will be with TBV now in play.

62295602
>>62292997
Yeah, that's it.

62295607
>>62272678
Under 20k? Are you sure?

62295716
>>62271712
>What’s stopping you from buying BTC?
the existence of Monero

62295989
>>62274282
Why the hate

62296012
>>62295607
sure under 20k
why whats your get back in number?

62297371
>>62296012
Not under 20k

62297376
>>62295716
Ah privacy ok ok

item.title: Kek XLM is absolutely ripping and is up multiple places overtaking monero and link while stinkies are at $8.99 LMAO HAHA… — 56 replies — 224.0/hr
item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-06-01 05:18 UTC

OP

LMAO HAHAHAHAHAHA OH NO NO LINKIES OH NO NO

62287876
>>62287844
we aren't cuckolds, okay? We just like knowing that Sergey keeps getting richer while Chainlink is stuck at 2020 prices

62288485
kek stinkies

62288499
>>62287844
xlm always does this before the bull..

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62290559
>>62287844
MOM IT'S DOING IT AGAIN

62290596
>>62289745
kek see this >>62290247

Not only are they going to be buying Link tokens, but they'll be buying Link tokens in order to pay for the gas on the corresponding blockchain. Because the fee in Link takes care of the gas.

62290754
>>62288539
I’ve fucked two guys wives before and filmed it

Idk what possesses dudes to be into this kind of shit but I got paid so

62290965
Xlm will actually hit $1

62291397
>xlm +43%
Damn linkies are fucking cooked.. when's the last time link had a pump like that? 2019??

62291419
>xlm up 100%
Kek linkies

62291508
>Almost in the top #10

Kek stinkies wish they had this kind of performance

62291680
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOm

62291689
>>62287844
Wow XLM sure is running! It must be so much higher than it was a year ago, right?????

62291699
>>62291680
so bullish chainlink

>>62291689
KABOOOOOM!

62291706
>>62291508
>pay tens of millions to Chainlink per year so you can be part of SCALE
>DTCC announces they will be using Chainlink to bridge tokenized assets to XLM using CCIP and exclusively using CRE for orchestration

>LINK -9%
>XLM +80%

62291709
>>62291706
Say it with me

The ... of crypto

62291787
>>62289658
>yet this is how the market reacts.
>No wonder crypto is getting is ass beat by the stock market so badly.

62291789
>>62291787
>market is never wrong

Thats why nothing has ever changed prices in history of human kind

62291989
>>62291787
Stocks are high in the sky and crypto is in the crapper, despite mass institutional adoption finally starting.

Stock market = right
Crypto market = wrong

62292014
>>62291709
THE cuckolds of crypto

62292427
>>62288957
> Big wallets keep accumulating and stacklets keep selling

Can you give a credible source that backs up your claims of whales accumulating and small wallets capitulating

62292431
KABOOOOOOOOOOM

62292813
>>62291989
>>62291789
My nw is 90% in crypto.
The market valuation of chainlink however, is 100% correct.

62293352
Xlm bros…
We got too cocky

62293596
>>62290228
Extremely based

62293668
>>62289756
>>62291706
More demonstration of muh price feeds meaning nothing to token holders. You were always wrong.

62294176
>>62287844
KABOOOOOOOM

62294523
Bullish XLM

62294622
>>62287844
Chainlink connects Stellar! Using Stellar means they are using Chainlink

62294685
Up we go

62295250
BOOOOOOOOOM

62295448
>>62294622
oh look, it's a video of a bird talking to a gay guy in grandma glasses
naturally this makes me want to buy altcoins

62295533
>>62295448
>naturally this makes me want to buy altcoins
Ok the video is about Chainlink, think about buying that too

62297380
So bullish

item.title: I sold all my XRP to buy XLM. I'm into crypto to make money, not just stare at charts that do nothing. — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I sold all my XRP to buy XLM. I'm into crypto to make money, not just stare at charts that do nothing.

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-01 05:19 UTC

OP

I sold all my XRP to buy XLM. I'm into crypto to make money, not just stare at charts that do nothing.

Early replies (first 3)

62292303
>>62292300
BOOOOOOOOM

62292328
>sell low
>buy high
the bizraeli way

62292649
>>62292300
I shorted the top of xlm last night
Then I bought xrp
Thanks for the liquidity

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62292860
>>62292300
>sold xrp for xrp lite
i wish you luck anon

62293818
>>62292300
Sucks to be you man. Check out YoungHoon Kim, worlds highest IQ. He has been talking about how ripple is going to be big the past few days.

https://x.com/yhbryankimiq/status/2060852640406487131

Not saying he is a authority on what wins and does not however its a factor on why I hold. Also XRP is at its floor right now and basicly a giant discount. Buy and hold and maybe 10 years from now it will be 100x. Not missing out on that

62294234
>>62292300
>>62292303
>>62292328
>>62292649
>>62292860
>>62293818
bullish xrp

62295064
>>62292300

62295072

delusional

62295451

item.title: ZEC HYPE SOL that's all you need to go to the moon. — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ZEC HYPE SOL that's all you need to go to the moon.

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-01 05:08 UTC

OP

ZEC HYPE SOL
that's all you need to go to the moon.

Early replies (first 3)

62297245
shut up nazi

62297258
>>62297236
Yes, these just pumped a few days ago.

62297267
Solana is going to $30 tho

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62297285
>>62297258
HYPE broke out of its previous ATH and is currently in a state of seeking a fair value price. It could unironically 20x from here in the next 6 months. Hype's mcap is only around 17 billion. Companies like Micron were already worth 240 billion in december when they began their generational run to 1.1 trillion now. I think people don't really respect how far shit can grow when people are speculating on the fair value of what will be the largest perpdex in the world once Trump's SEC legalizes perpdexes.

62297286
>>62297285
Yeah, buybuybuy!

62297302
>>62297286
This but unironically. FOR FUCKS SAKE /BIZ/ JUST GET ON THE ELEVATOR NIGGERS!

62297354
>>62297285
thanks just shorted

item.title: Saturday's almost over, wagie. Tomorrow you'll be too depressed about the coming week to enjoy your time off, so your we… — 31 replies — 27.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Saturday's almost over, wagie. Tomorrow you'll be too depressed about the coming week to enjoy your time off, so your we…

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-06-01 04:11 UTC

OP

Saturday's almost over, wagie. Tomorrow you'll be too depressed about the coming week to enjoy your time off, so your weekend is already spiritually finished. What's that? You didn't come any closer to finding a way out of wagie hell this weekend? OOPS, I guess you'll have to try again next weekend HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62293547
wdym depressed
if im not at work i literally dont know what do do with myself, i just shitpost on /smg/ while waiting for the market to open
I wish I could work 7 days a week, more money I could throw in the market

62293578
>>62293533
I don't even know who these threads are for. Are there /biz/ who work at amazon warehouses? I feel like that would be rare.

62293839
>>62293533
This has to be the real Bezos. Who else could be this consistent?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62293843
>>62293533
you're such an asshole lol

62293898
>>62293533
Well at least I still have both my parents. You don't have your mother who u cried like a bitch at her death after being a dementia vegetable for so long. While she was alive she gazed at you with animal like uncomprehending eyes and u had to gaze back and realize that mom u knew literally doesn't exist

62293907
>>62293533
your rocket ejaculated too early now cleanup that mess Jeffy

62293911
finally we have an answer to him

62293954
>>62293908
Paid for by wagies

62293966
>>62293954
in the end, everything is paid for by wagies, comrade
but in the immediate reckoning, this is on bezos 100%. on his company to be precise, which is a large fraction of his nw
wagies still get their wages even if the rocket goes boom

62293968
>>62293533
at least im not married to some bimbo spic slut

62293969
>>62293533
Saturday to recover
Sunday to prepare
A wagie's weekend vanishes into thin air!

62293976
plz stop mr. bezos

62293981
Every time a bezos thread is posted his start flexing that u have a mother who is alive and doesn't have dementia

62294021
>>62293908
KEK oh nononono bezosissies....

62294023
>>62293533
Was a nice four day week last week wasn't it, wagie. Now you're staring down the barrel of a fresh five

62294025
>>62293908
You will have the incident investigation finalised and on my desk Monday morning sharp, won't you wagie.

62294069
>>62293533
I swear its the real Bezos posting these

62294070
>>62293533
Jokes on you, I'm prepping my resignation letter.

62294254
>>62294069
What else would you do with his wealth? It gives you unlimited power to humiliate wagies.

62294795
>>62293533
It's ok, next week is another week. I will flip so many burgers, I swear. Only 10000 more years of saving 100k a year until I become a billionaire like you, master.

The hopium is real.

62295016
>>62293908
clean it up, wagie
>>62293954
>Paid for by wagies
this is how the jew make his money

62295035
>>62293839
Possibly, in any case he is right

62295099
>>62293578
I've always wondered about this. Also most wages don't get the whole weekend off, they usually get a random day off in the week

62295165
I love the pure seethe that originates as a result of this sentiment. Wagies beg for opportunities to be made slaves and then get upset at the realization that they are nothing but. This system is the greatest in the world and the mass of tireless goyim work wonderfully. Another week off for me!

62296661
>>62293839
I've always wondered if it was the real Bezos. Chances are very slim, but not impossible.

62296690
>>62294069
Well nobody is real especially not him.

62296781
>>62294069
I feel like the pictures would be better if so .he would drop some exclusive rare ones like at a party with weinstein cackling at some ass to ass

62297163
>>62293578
I worked there for the last 8 months before my current wfh job

62297194
>>62293533
The first half of your shift is paying for the fuel to commute to the wage cage, the second half is tax. It's alright wagie, tomorrow you'll start earning for yourself.

62297257
>>62293533
Keep hustlin' wagie, get that bread fr fr no cap

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 75 replies — 16.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 75 • Updated: 2026-06-01 00:44 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62286076
>>62286025
It's nowhere close to where I'd feel comfortable charging people money for it. Trade bots/API feeds will probably be subscription, when that's done, but those are more for pro traders
There's also daily updates on the twitter account

62286347
>>62285878
>outputs start to get a little blurry 4-5 weeks out and then just turn into noise, which is why I do the updates every 4 weeks
Why not update weekly? or even daily?

62286447
>>62286347
>Why not update weekly? or even daily?
Basically, because it's a lot easier - there are a lot of inputs into the model that each have to be harvested and processed by me before they get fed into the sim. It runs into the hours each time. I can also cope that the current date's "conditions" are obviously the starting point of the forecast, so having them flattening out a little bit is easier to align
I do weekly updates on the site and the twitter account, but that's 90% just applying current price momentum and FX momentum to the existing physics-based forecast, which I have to get better at. I'm a nerd, not a trader

62286532
>>62284814
>buy may 17
>sell for a loss on May 25

thanks

62286552
>>62286532
>buy may 17
>short may 25
you're welcome

62286639
>>62286447
>because it's a lot easier
Fair enough. Although I'm sure at least some of that data gathering and processing could be automated.
>I'm a nerd, not a trader
So you don't use it all? I get that some people are very risk averse but if you're confident in your technology I don't see why you wouldn't at least dip your toes and make what is essentially free money.

I think I might do a bit of paper trading and I'll let you know how it pans out by the end of June.

62286700
>>62286639
>Fair enough. Although I'm sure at least some of that data gathering and processing could be automated.
A lot of it is! I keep getting tempted to outsource some of it to an LLM but some gut instinct says "you'd be really really dumb to do that," even a local one
>So you don't use it all? I get that some people are very risk averse but if you're confident in your technology I don't see why you wouldn't at least dip your toes and make what is essentially free money.
Oh, I use it, have been trading perps with it for years, getting more profitable as the model evolves. After the addition of the FX layer last spring, I can get away with some truly disgusting leverage. I just meant I don't have a background in trading or technical analysis, so I've been learning all of those skills on the go. It's all related but still a different skillset
>I think I might do a bit of paper trading and I'll let you know how it pans out by the end of June.
Please do, I'll post in here when there's updates but you can always get in touch via Twitter

62287275
>>62284814
I’m just gonna wait for the mega dump to flush the longs down to 60k..

62289468
>>62284814
L, and let me be perfectly clear, FG

62289671
>>62289468
So up from here? Don’t speak zoomzoom

62289838
>>62289671
This is where price should turn up - hybrid FX+physics says Buy because FX is so positive, physics officially flips green tomorrow, it all seems pretty good for bulls over the next 24 hours.

62289954
>>62287275
this. I just go all in bottom of the bear market and do nothing a couple years

62291704
>>62284814
This is no proper backtesting! You must test at least 30 instances!
https://youtu.be/zXoZdnYfIlA?si=MPB49se3bA1H2fnp

62291711
>>62291704
*32 Instances

62291719
>>62291711
>>62291704
?

62291779
>>62291719
How big are the wins and how big are the losses? They have to be in a profitable proportion together with the win rate. 66% win rate with wins 1/3 as big as the losses is not profitable for example.

62291951
>>62284814
broken clock o algo

62292012
what time of day do you make the trades

62292563
if you have a profitable strategy then just make a hyperliquid vault so we can join it and you have 10% profit share

62292730
>>62291779
There's a whole website and twitter account where you can check these things
>>62292012
>a broken clock predicts the future 80% of the time
That's one hell of a broken clock
>>62292012
The demo strategy executes ~2 hours into NY open, when there's enough FX data to confirm or inval the signal
>>62292563
Down the list of things to do. Right now it's a trade bot, which is being built because a mid-tier CEX wants to explore white labelling signals, so the bot needs to be optimized, but then I'll absolutely integrate it into HL

62292891
>>62292730
Thank you, I already have working strategies. I was just advocating doing proper backtesting before applying any strategy.

62292918
>>62292891
>I was just advocating doing proper backtesting before applying any strategy.
Always a good practice - prediction/outcome goes back to December 2024 which is where the hit rate is from. I could backtest to mid-2024 (when the FX layer was added) if I feel like it but I figured 100+ signals over more than a year was good enough

62293541
Spoonfeed me pls OP? Am I supposed to follow your twitter? How do I get the meme lines for myself.

62293559
>>62293541
>How do I get the meme lines for myself.
I'll post them in here, but there's also a live dashboard and a twitter, where a follow/likes would be greatly appreciated
>>62285878

62293571
>>62293559
Thanks for doing the needful sir. I'll be checking this out. Big ups anon.

62293619
>>62292730
Does the trading bot have contingencies in place just in case other trading bots start countertrading your strategy if it ends up being too successful?

62293680
>>62293619
I think if someone else's trading bot can move BTC by a few percent per day then we all have much bigger problems

62294595
>>62284814
Just buy the dip and sell the high simple. The main thing is to hold. I'm not only holding, I also staked some of my BTC recently since we can do that now without bridge involved.

62296711
BEHOLD, THE FUTURE

62296738
>>62296711

item.title: crypto shills destroyed /biz/ — 46 replies — 11.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
crypto shills destroyed /biz/

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-06-01 01:08 UTC

OP

you stupid shitcunts nobody is going to buy your fucken dumb digital coin that has no fucken use case

You were too late for bitcoin

you got psychologically destroyed by millennial and gen x goobers posting on twitter from their rented condos in miami

And now you're here trying to tiktok dance your way into fucken millions

GTFO

Early replies (first 3)

62293753
>>62293749
don't you have some dying boomer business to take care of lol

62293756
>>62293753
wowee

*does the fortnite dance*

feed my more low i.q. shortform slop sir!

Imagine being a GEN Z male who cannot even focus long enough to fill out a fucken business plan

62293763
>>62293756
imagine being so small brained that you have to work with dirty pipes since its the only way you wont lose everything. its indeed better you leave the crypto to smarter people and focus on the piss and shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62293926
>>62293876
also the stealth thing is pretty smart desu. lowers vectors of attack against you. status signalling does have some practical uses but you probably already are smart enough to know when and where to flaunt your status.

62293934
>>62293920
anyone who is spiritually miami deserved to be crushed, that is true.

i'm just some bloke who used tism pattern recognition and intuition to financially help himself and his family. i'm not sorry for any of it and i'd do it all again. i pay my tax like a good goy and don't have to wagie because of this, literally every day i'm free to do as i please. it was worth it desu. take risks in life. doesn't have to be crypto, fuck crypto if you hate it. just bet on yourself in some avenue of life. risk it all.

62293943
>>62293934
for sure, those are great points. And well done!

62294000
>>62293753
What's a non boomer business?

62294194
Chainlink solves multiple real world problems. If you're too dumb to see that, maybe lurk a little longer and read more.
More generally, cryptography, and the financial instruments being developed that are secured by it, strengthens capitalism. It gives more tools to more people and increases the efficiency of the economy. Here's a really simple example for you: tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. This will enable the whole world to have exposure to hard money. If you can't appreciate that, you should suck your double barrel that's on your nightstand and get the fuck off of my board you decrepit, balding abomination

62294197
>>62293749
Ok newfag

62294203
>>62293749
The wirld is only getting more digital. It only makes sense that digital assets would raise in value. Right now it's a free for all casino. Take it or leave it this is the way it is.

62294208
>>62293826
>i've roundtripped low 7figs
>pokemon cards
The only thing you've roundtripped is the methheads behind the dumpster.

62294210
>>62293749
t top buying baggie.

62294219
>>62294208
>>pokemon cards
i think you're confusing anon here, i never mentioned anything about trading cards.

62294221
>>62294219
My mistake. You are still a homosexual zoomer for investing in shitcoins.

62294231
>>62294221
wow, that was a very rude thing to say.

62294248
>>62293749
>crypto shills destroyed /biz/
/biz/ was created specifically to contain crypto shilling

62294250
>>62294194
>Chainlink solves multiple real world problems.
stopped reading right there, baggie

62294305
>>62294194
>Here's a really simple example for you: tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. This will enable the whole world to have exposure to hard money
Explain what this means. Seriously, explain it. Tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. How is that any different than buying 'gold' on an exchange that's just a certificate? They're both digital, but one of them is just stored on a rube-goldberg mechanism instead of a simple database. How does it enable "the whole world" to have exposure to hard money?

You're just saying fucking words.

62294322
>>62294305
>how are tokenized assets different from legacy assets??

t.

62294326
>>62294322
Yes. In a qualitative way, not a quantitative way, explain how they are fundamentally different. Being stored on a slightly different digital substrate doesn't change : you hold a -promise- that you own gold, not actual physical gold.

62294328
>>62294326
Ask your local AI fuckhead.
Nobody here wants to explain the absolute basics of tokenization to you, even the banks have been trying to explain the benefits for over half a decade.

62294331
>>62294328
I want to see you explain it, because you can't, without regurgitating another explanation that will be fille with just as much of a smokescreen.

62294337
>>62294331
listen to yourself

62294340
>>62294337
>a really simple example for you
You're the one who said it. If it's so simple, surely you can bang out a coherent explanation in a minute or two.

You're a tech fetishist. You are mentally bound to this retarded world because you imagine it makes you smart, but it's really just smart in the "grandma thinks he's good with computer" kind of way.

62294365
>>62294340
Take a deep breath you fat boomer, your bp is spiking.

62294367
>>62293749
These sluts must know they are creating wankmaterial. They arent fooling anyone. Skanks. I hate it when they trick my brain into wanting to put my penis inside them. They would be raped 24/7 in the old days.

62294368
>>62294365
I accept your concession.

62294378
>>62293749
That's not even her real body. The app program she's using starts stuttering to keep up as she turns to the side. She's probably built like a soup can.

62294385
>>62293749
use cases are bearish desu

62294446
>>62294385
Probably why memes did so well last cycle. Why i am buying blue chip memes again while they are cooked.

62294649
>>62293756
can you clean my pipe?

62294739
>>62293749
I was at a Calgary Flames hockey game last year and there was a boomer ahead of me who must have drank 7 or 8 tallboys and was just watching shit like this on his phablet.

Sometimes you gotta respect the absolute zero regard boomers have.

62296803
>>62294194
nobody wants hard money lmfao
soft money is how the system works because they can just run w/e fucken scams they want
the system isn't for the people brother
gold was done away with for this exact reason

item.title: I made a drink that was revealed to me in a dream. How do I sell it? — 33 replies — 6.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
I made a drink that was revealed to me in a dream. How do I sell it?

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-06-01 00:30 UTC

OP

It's one of the most delicious things I've ever had. Genuinely top 10 drinks. I wanna sell it, but... How? Who do I pitch this to? How tf do you market "this recipe was revealed to me in a dream?" Everyone who's tried it loves it but IDK where to go from here.

Early replies (first 3)

62290943
>>62290941
moon brew...

62290953
>>62290941
go to pepsico
walk in the front door
find the owner
stand up straight, look him in the eye, give him a firm handshake
tell him you had a dream about a drink and pepsis and money
????
profit

62290960
give me the recipe and i'll tell you if it's actually good. i have god tier taste buds and taste in general. . Normies fear my eloquent opinions.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62290979
>>62290960
I'm absolutely not giving up the recipe. Dream your own drinks.
>>62290953
It's gotta be firm or it doesn't work

62291614
If it has to be made fresh? Form an LLC, save up enough money to rent out a small store or street facing bar/stand whatever with signage out front etc. and make it known locally. If you are truly destitute poor then set up a table at all the local farmers markets in every town within 5 hours of you and sell it there. Hire hot women to sell it at these stands/markets/stores once you've saved enough money then get it to start trending on social media short form videos hiring hot girls and guys to drink it in videos so people will travel there to try it

62291617
>>62291614
if it's any good it would take professional tasters about 3 minutes to figure out exactly how to copy it and set up competing businesses.

that's the problem with food in the US. You can't actually stop rivals from copying your recipe

62291622
>>62291617
add some natural food coloring to it to throw them off for a few more years. Doesn't need to be blue or something just make it the wrong neutral natural looking color. OP i want to taste your delicious drink

62291625
>>62291622
lol

changing the color isn't going to throw off anyone that tastes food for a living

and that just makes it easier to copy
>OP sues competitor for copying his drink
>but yours is blue, ours isn't!
>lawsuit gets tossed
>OP dies in a back alley for lack of a working business model

62291633
>>62291625
this is like saying you should never start a business because there are other businesses out there so don't try. There aren't going to be professional taste testers at the farmers market trying to set up another beverage table lmao. Even if there were, it will take years for a major business to roll out their copy to the masses going through all the legal trouble and logistics and figuring out how to get it shelf stable in bottle and OP will have already made good money off it by then just like what happens to every good new product

62291638
>>62291633
I'm talking about the point where he's renting buildings and setting up new locations

at that point mcdonalds starbucks and about 5000 other bars and restaurants are going to send tasters out and in a matter of weeks will have sourced bulk ingredients, safety and marketing tested the drinks, and implemented them across thousands of existing stores

>how to get it shelf stable in bottle
that's not how restaurants and bars in the US sell things.

62291641
>>62291638
Yeah I can see that happening but that will take time, he still has time to make money from it before then, and if he wants he still has a decent shot at marketing his street facing walk up bar as having "the original moon brew" or whatever and can still make some money. What I'm saying is that he should still try so that I can taste this thing

62291647
>>62291641
Yes, it can be done. He's probably going to have to sell to a competitor eventually though I'd guess. Or take on investors to get the capital to go big.

I'm lazy, I'd just take the recipe directly to someone that already has the marketing and locations to produce it in place right off the bat.

62291649
>>62291647
those are all good ideas and would make money from a dream which is pretty funny, i'm rooting for you OP it better not be chocolate milk with saffron added or some shit

62291651
>>62291649
>it better not be chocolate milk with saffron added or some shit
kek'd out loud

I bet it is.

>Orange juice with vanilla, milk, and an egg made billions

62291662
>>62290941
you dont unless your dream told you how to sell it.

you will fail

62291664
>>62291651
kek now i want an orange julius god this thread makes me thirsty, i hope it's something strange like that

62293191
>>62290941
you made chocolate milk that looks like barf and want to sell it?

62293358
>>62291617
you say that, but nobody has managed to figure out coke's recipe in almost a century.

62293376
>>62293358
have you ever tried store-brand colas?

62293478
>>62290941
It’s got cum in it, doesn’t it?

62293488
>>62290941
thanks for this anon, just made a drink of orange, lime juice, beet powder, sugar, carbonated raspberry water
maybe I'll sell it, top 10 fruity drink for sure

62293646
>>62290941
I have that glass.

62293674
>>62290941
What's in it as ingredients, not a combo? What will you call it so we can know how to look for it?

62293703
>>62290941
There was some fucktard on biz way back when it was a new board, I think his name was Riley when he used a trip, and he kept posting about his invention that would revolutionize kitchens around the world. Everyone would have one. He wouldn’t tell us what it did but believed very strongly in it and just wanted help patenting or licensing his invention. Well before ally spilled the beans as they say and posted a retarded MS Paint drawing of his retarded invention: a tortilla chip warmer. Like a low powered air fryer basically.
We all laughed and then he never posted again.

62293708
>>62291651
Orange whip? Orange whip?

Three orange whips.

62293779
>>62290941
>cocoa milk with banana

62294310
>>62291614
>>62291649
Thank you for the based advice :)
Yes, it's a real multi-step recipe
>>62293358
They have https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TDkH3EbWTYc
>>62293703
lmao

62294321
>>62293358
That's not true at all. There are like 10 different versions of coke, Pepsi, mountain dew and doctor pr in the us

62294379
>>62294310
happy to help screenshot those posts so you don't forget and keep us updated I'm sure your drink is mighty tasty

62294400
>>62290941

You can't trademark a recipe. You have to open up a restaurant and start selling Flaming Moes or you can sell the recipe to a restaurateur.

62294504
>>62290941
>OP invents horchata

You should market it by opening a drink kiosk somewhere with lots of foot traffic. Call it "San Diego's own MOONJUICE" or wherever you live/whatever you name it. Nobody cares how it was invented but if you must use that in the pitch, you can post a blurb on the side of your kiosk about it being the traditional drink of some Seussian fantasy country from which you claim your grandfather was an immigrant, who passed down the recipe generation to generation.

Best of luck OP. I like your initiative.

62296684
>>62290941
is this you

item.title: >Saturday has already started You have less than 48 hours to scrabble desperately for a way out of your wagie cage befor… — 14 replies — 3.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Saturday has already started You have less than 48 hours to scrabble desperately for a way out of your wagie cage befor…

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-01 00:47 UTC

OP

>Saturday has already started

You have less than 48 hours to scrabble desperately for a way out of your wagie cage before you have to go back inside for five days. But you're too tired and have too many chores to do to make ANY progress. You're stuck. STUCK. HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

Early replies (first 3)

62291831
>>62291829
yeaaaah, i'm starting a new cage job this Monday. I don't wanna do it. also I'm getting a reply on fucking monday whether my consultancy gig is going through, so on fucking Monday while waging I know whether I shouldn't had even showed up.
>Grim

62291832
Its the King's Birthday on monday so i shant be working in the goy factory

62291837
>>62291832
Doom island, crack a beer for me cunt

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62291846
>>62291829

The freshly severed LVHC needed to be disposed of. The severance package entailed a quick, fully paid departure if they boarded the rocket.

62291922
>>62291829
the rocket isn't gonna fix itself. Clean it up wagie and then back in your cagie!

62292383
What? I have a 12 hour shift in a kitchen today.

62292615
OP is a retarded faggot, this nigger will never stop bezos posting on this board

62293054
Worked in the wage cage for about a week when I was 19 and addicted to heroin, spent most of the day jerking off or playing runescape2. Good times

62293068
>>62291829
bezos is next

62294027
>>62291829
Scrabble is my favorite board game.

62294029
>>62291829
>OP isnt spending time with his gf on the weekend

62294903
>>62291846
imagine making that a poster and putting on the wall of an amazon warehouse. lol do you thing the wagies will gain consciousness

62294907
I own my own businesses, get fucked corpo

62296747
>>62291829
How old are you again Jeff? You better enjoy those next 15 years. Instead of blowing rockets into the athmosphere, you should better try to help the poor. Or maybe you think you are irredeemable.

item.title: Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet? — 26 replies — 104.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet?

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-01 05:16 UTC

OP

Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet? Allow me to explain.
Cryptocurrencies support decentralized networks, which are used for data computation and storage. These networks are cheaper to use than traditional server infrastructure.
If it's cheaper, why don't corporations use them? One, there are regulations preventing adoption, but CLARITY will change this in the US. Two, corporations have invested trillions into building and maintaining server infrastructure. Liquidation is risky from there point of view because they don't understand decentralized networks. A few brave corporations will lead this great transition, and when they are successful, everyone will follow.
You are in the final window of being early. Don't fuck this up.

Early replies (first 3)

62292772
>>62292765
cope

62292786
>>62292765
How much does 1 Gigabyte on the Bitcoin Blockchain cost?

62292794
>>62292786
>thinks a blockchain is the only data structure for distributed storage

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62292827
too bad clarity won't pass

62292899
CLARITY won't change a thing, the public's appetite for crypto is gone.

62292905
>>62292899
CLARITY is not for the public. It's for corporations.

62294414
>>62292765
>These networks are cheaper to use than traditional server infrastructure.
Beguiled that you would say this with a straight face.

62294730
It's slowly and steadily coming.
t. takes well over a million in cc payments a year.
The "demand" isnt' from pleb retards swiping their cards. It's from business.
We've all moved to ACH/check for all wholesale tx and it's a big swing the last two or three years.
Tough times make people get pissed.
Businesses will get their cheap processing by 2030 I'd guess.

62295598
>>62294414
What? Are you retarded?

62295608
>>62294730
Exactly.
>Businesses will get their cheap processing by 2030 I'd guess.
I'm anticipating 2028.

62295772
>>62292765
Cool clock Achmed.

62295815
>>62292905
There is no reason whatsoever for them to buy your token. Even Chainlink. They sold you tokens to raise funds, companies won’t need to pay. They made this clear many times including just recently.

62295887
>>62295815
Are you aware of how annoying credit card fees are for businesses? Do you know how much it costs to build and maintain a server room? Crypto saves businesses money.

62295975
>>62295598
Do you have any idea how much data is processed in the world? In the big data world, there's this concept of "scale factor". Scale factor 1 is a gigabyte. So scale factor 1000 would be a terabyte. Things don't even start to get interesting until you're looking at scale factor 10000. A fairly modest, multiple-times-a-day job for a single company is not uncommonly something like a terabyte. Huge companies can run jobs in the petabyte range. Every single one of these is using an insanely optimized query engine (Hadoop, Spark, etc) running across hundreds or even thousands of nodes that are optimized down to the hardware level. Why do they do this? I'll give you a hint - it's not speed they care about. They optimize for cost. They want to get everything as cheap as possible. A couple of retarded desktops spread around the world isn't going to be 'cheaper'. It would take you two weeks just to LOAD a petabyte of memory, let alone process it.

It's hilarious how completely clueless you idiots are about what constitutes "compute". The kinds of things you're thinking about are below toy level.

62296016
>>62295975
I'm aware of the scaling. DePIN is scalable to match and eventually exceed the processing and storage capabilities of servers.
Cute post.

62296032
>>62296016
Ridiculous. A group of randomly connected, disparate pieces of hardware will never even come close to the capacity (or usage price) of dedicated, tuned hardware. You're being defrauded with these promises.

62296045
>>62296032
You are an old man, history will bury you...

62296210
>>62296032
lol

62296324
>>62292765
it took decades for the internet to really be adopted and it still hasn't been fully realized

62296340
>>62295887
Incredible amounts of money over time and it applies to the little guys even more than the big guys. Volume does come through little guys though and they will be included.
Biggest boon in decades for online and local business is coming.

62296419
>>62292827
I am calling it, clarity act will pass, despite the bots online telling you it won't. The BTC halving is under 2 years away, they're squeezing the stock market right now and then smart money rotates elsewhere to make tenbaggers.
All Congress and US politicians are bought by Israel, if you aren't profiting off of this than shame on you. Everyone across the internet will tell you crypto is over forever, and the clarity act won't pass.
Meanwhile look how much politicians own stock in MARA or MSTR. Even trump owns these two.
MARA only owns two assets by the way, bitcoin and Kaspa (Jewish trilemma solving nu coin)

62296587
>>62296419
>then my real life can begin

62296607
>>62292765
Crypto will take many decades to be adopted, and several centuries to become dominant, so we're as early as possible.

We're the bastards that were around when BTC was only $73,000.

62296629
>>62292765
In order to use somebody else's compute, you need to compensate them fairly for it. The math will never work when you consider the price advantage large companies have when buying hardware at scale. Also, they technically already do decentralization with CDNs.

62296638
>>62292765
It doesnt offer a solution to a problem that exists.

62297373
>>62296629
For a huge amount of the business world, actual compute isn't even the real issue. It's interconnects. Data movement. When you're breaking up a 10 terabyte job across a thousand nodes, you are very very rarely bottlenecked by compute. You're bottlenecked by shuffling the data between machines. The whole reason massive datacenters exist is because they can use insane interconnects (1TB, even 10 TB/s fabric). Using retarded internet shitcoin routing mechanisms to attempt this is like using a garden hose to put out a lava flow.

item.title: The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies — 69 replies — 39.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies

Open thread • Replies: 69 • Updated: 2026-06-01 03:35 UTC

OP

BAT bros? You still there? What the fuck is going on? LINK shits. BTC dumps deadly diarrhea. Orange man shitposts. But this piece of shit is +15%? Is Lobes flipping the switch? Hold me. Also. Jennie lewds please

Early replies (first 3)

62285628
no one still owns BAT
... right?

62285632
>>62285628
Apparently I do. Jennie doesn't though

62285713
YOU KNOW THE DEAL.

TP - WHERE ARE MY THOTS AT? IT'S BEEN A WHILE.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62290812
>>62290702
Seems to be doing a lot better. I really appreciate it!
>HK
sick! I might be going to Shanghai sometime later in the Summer. If I make it out there, I'll let you know. Hope you have a comfy stay!

>>62290728
>>62290732
I got the name for you >>62286122
Cece Rose. I'll dump more of the OF if you don't want to pay for it.

_
Might get a vacay for this one, but fuck it.

62290849
>>62290812
cool TP
yeah let me know
this apartment is way better than the last place I was assigned thank fuck
kekeke we'll see you next thread ig but worth it lel
jannies might have chilled tho I hope

62290910
>>62290812
LEGEND!

I'm gonna need the name of this goth whore next. Are those braces?

62291155
>>62290355
>>62290662
>>62290812
Fucking hell, TP. More of this please, especially >>62290355

62291188
>>62285628
Nope. Sold everything when it became clear they’re not actually interested in building utility for the token. It’s a memecoin connected to a browser. They had the world by the balls and they made a conscious decision to do absolutely nothing for BAT. They cannot be trusted

62291222
>>62290355
You're a pornographer, the fuck you care?

62291254
>>62286450
>>62290355
>>62290662
>>62290812
TP MOGS /gif/
THE MOTHERFUCKING GOAT

62291656
>>62290812
Wonderful rack on her. Lets see some asian MILFS GILFS

62291660
>>62287337
she's hands down the hottest crypto girl, the hottest thing about her is that she hides how big her ass is, won't even show it off out of modesty

62291667
>>62289174
Most BAT holders moved on to something else eventually, but still hold some BAT out of loyalty and the purest copium you'll ever find

Anyone who was "all-in" on BAT has sold or killed themselves by now

62292238
>>62286450
need more of this one lads

62292673
>>62291660
i want to see her tiddies so bad

62292929
>>62290849
>this apartment is way better than the last place I was assigned
I'm happy for you ^_^!

>>62290910
>Are those braces?
Yes sir!

>>62291155
Czech'd and I should be around to post more later tonight for you.

>>62291254
That means more than you know, lad! Thanks for the love <3

>>62291656
>Lets see some asian MILFS GILFS
Short on time rn, but if you want something better than this, lmk and I'll see what I can do later.

62293156
>>62292929
>Czech'd and I should be around to post more later tonight for you.

oh baby. Trans jans are letting TP poast freely for once in a blue moon, love to see it

62293250
>>62285628
i've still got 20k BAT, might swap for LINK if it pumps

62293268
>>62290812
that's def a prosthetic vest

62293317
>>62293268
You’re a retard

62293375
>>62293317
im right faggot

62293393
>>62293375
Keep telling yourself nigger

62293407
>>62292929
>tfw no chink grandma gf

62294009
bat is the biggest fucking let down in the crypto world a fucking regret ever fucking hearing about bat

62294011
and faggot brendan is piece of shit con man no better than a jeet

62294012
>>62294009
>a fucking regret ever fucking hearing about bat

We all do, these threads are our group therapy cope session. Welcome

62294071
>>62294009
Dude just let go sell it for something that will actually go up in price. I know it’s hard but if you actually look around there’s companies out there that are still actually looking to improve peoples lives thru crypto with actual utility unlike BAT which is a nothing more than a glorified memecoin. VVV and RAIL are two perfect examples that are mark to market. There’s more than that. Think ai, privacy, stablecoins and robotics. BAT is fucking cooked

62295627
>>62292929
Get back here TP we need moarrrrrrrrr thots

62296078
>>62294011
birendra eichwala and his do not redeem token

62296132

62296359
>>62294009
>>62294011
we should sue him for false advertising and stealing investors money

62297106
tps last post got wiped out. It was fun while it lasted

62297187
>>62296359
yes

62293848

62293858

62293889

62295399

62296332

62296342

62296605

>32

62296681
>>62293913

Anyway keep going and good luck managing your wealth.

62297312
>>62293913

item.title: This is the only crypto in existence with not only hype/momentum (no pun intended) but a very serious value proposition. — 31 replies — 14.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
This is the only crypto in existence with not only hype/momentum (no pun intended) but a very serious value proposition.

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-06-01 03:11 UTC

OP

This is the only crypto in existence with not only hype/momentum (no pun intended) but a very serious value proposition. And if you're a stockfag it should worry you that HYPE is positioned to eat your lunch too.

Early replies (first 3)

62294797
>>62294791
Ur mom is hyping on my dick rn saar

62294824
>>62294797
Holy based

62295023
>>62294791
/biz/ decided this coin was a bad investment so it’s probably got another 10x in the tank

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62295124
make a couple more threads, keep mentioning chainlink and how it sucks
It's not obvious at all lmao

62295182
>>62295124
LINK is shit-tier until proven otherwise. And the only way to prove it is price appreciation.

62295244
>>62295182
>link ico: 6c, now: $9, an increase of 150x
>your jeetcoin ico: $3, now: $68, 22x

62295256
>>62295244
Chainlink ICOd at .11

62295260
>>62295244
>150x
How long ago was that again?

62295267
>>62295244
>Jeetcoin airdrop: 33% of the entire supply to anyone who even logged in which would be >$20 billion right now
>Link: No airdrop, 500k weekly market sells until the end of time
>reserve went from novelty mechanism to the main driver behind token value accrual entirely because of hype

62295296
>>62295244
Hype had no ico retard. It was an airdrop

62295397
>>62295244
LINK ICO'd in 2017, there was much less money in crypto back then which is why its valuation was much lower.

62295441
>>62295397
Idk why you even reply to any Link bagholder, they are literally retarded and will defend their useless coin even if that means them being broke and homeless

62295526
>>62295441
they must capitulate and buy HYPE

62295594
>>62295526
It would be hilarious, in fact they would buy the top this year, not sell in 2029 and keep spamming Hype on this board. I'd love to dump my bags on them.

62295599
>>62294791
Basturd?

62295634
>>62295599
indians dont shill 15B MC coins, they shill 50k MC shitters. And they do it on twitter, indians abandoned this board a long time ago.

62295664
>>62294791
hype is great, you guys won, yes
but can it just fucking pullback so i can buy some

62295676
>>62295664
no, buy it now and pump the price up nigger, coins dont go up in price when a bunch of bitches are waiting for a pullback

62295683
>>62295676
kek, sound logic

62295693
HYPE is not a competitor to stocks, no one is invested directly in the NYSE, hype is a tool that allows you to leverage yourself to the tits to trade stocks at 2 AM on a saturday while drunk in order to exit all positions when Trump declares he's going to start sinking russian boats until they give up in ukraine and potentially start WW3.

62296001
>>62295023
Well i was mad, i decided to long the dip at 44 but then i was away and missed the pump.

62296011
>>62295244

I ONLY MADE 22x WAHHHHHH!!!!

Fuck you retards. I own link but linkies are actually insufferable. I hope Sergay rapes you in your sleep.

62296019
>HYPERLIQUID SURPASSES DOGECOIN TO BECOME THE 10TH LARGEST CRYPTO BY MARKET CAP
The Chinaman turns around and eats the Dog.

62296226
>>62296001
you didn't miss shit....yet....

62296308
>>62296019
It sucks that he's a chink, but at least he was born in the US

62296777
>open biz
>see multiple hype threads
Thanks for the top signal. RIP to the newfags who fall for it

62296784
>>62296308
Check out the Colossus interview he did. It gives a nice in-depth peek at how he and the rest of the team operates. Its cringe but that was my 'these chinks are the real deal' moment.

62297090
>>62296777
>777
Yeah cause ant sh4res was at it's peak when it was shilled heavily here, and countless other coins. You newfaggots are fucking retarded.

62297103
>>62296777

Look at that. On its way back down to $40

62297105

item.title: Why do workers/poor hate investing? — 70 replies — 8.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do workers/poor hate investing?

Open thread • Replies: 70 • Updated: 2026-05-31 21:30 UTC

OP

Most conversation with friends/family/coworkers I have when it comes to investing long term in Roth IRA, high yield savings, and my ETF Dividend portfolio even with all the money I make working cyber security utilizing FIRE method they get so aggressive about “what if” scenarios thinking I’m day-trading and even when I explain it they don’t get it.

“WHAT IF THE MARKET ALL GOES UNDER AND YOU LOSE EVERYTHING”

“WHAT IF YOU TAKE ALL YOUR MONEY OUT AND SELL ON A LOSS”

“WHAT IF SOMETHING BAD HAPPENS AND YOU HAVE NO MONEY”

Which I retort what happens when you lose your job? When the market goes under and suddenly you’re up for layoffs?

“WELL THAT WILL NEVER HAPPEN BECAUSE IM VALUABLE AND MY SKILLS AND MY TIME AR COMPANY AND-“

Like do these people really think working for someone for the rest of your life is the only way to live? I’d rather live frugally but free, and that really seems to irk people.


What the hell is this psychology? Why do they hate the idea someone doesn’t wanna work for someone for the rest of their life or majority? Do we forget money is to buy our freedom?

Early replies (first 3)

62294872
They are physically, mentally incapable of visualizing what you are trying to explain in terms of long term time horizons, compounding returns, etc. It's not worth bothering with. Like trying to talk to a dog.

62294877
There's a reason poor "people" are poor
The final redpill and the toughest to swallow is that jews are 100% right about the goyim

62294881
>>62294870
You probably come across as obnoxious and irritating and you're clearly in your early 20s and lack the maturity to keep shit to yourself. People don't just randomly get aggressive the way you've described, you're missing the key details and you're probably rubbing people up the wrong way and saying all the wrong this because you lack tact and grace.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62295158
>>62294980
>seething this hard
You lost and lost hard, you eternal child. Go cry someplace that cares.

62295173
>>62295155
I recently saw a YouTube video where a guy showed what it would look like if you invested $1,000 every month into the S&P500 if you bought at the absolute worst time (highest price) for each month for seven years. It still made a ton of money. Even if you had the worst luck in human history and chose the worst day to invest every single time you still come out ahead.

62295177
>>62294881

>Bro i know you barely make ends meet already, but if you put 90% of your income into #FARTPEPE you'll be rich bro! I made a killing on it using mommy's creditcard that one time. Why are you so angry bro, don't you want to be a smart investor like me? Why do you hate investing? Why are you subhuman worker trash incapable of understanding simple financial advice? I'm just trying to help!

62295192
>>62295155
And when the price is down, like in 2015 where gold dropped down to $1,000 after a peak around $1,700, it’s "I’m not buying this shit lol". Whatever happens, there’s always a good excuse to do nothing.

62295196
>>62295173
>worst luck in human history
>in the longest and biggest bull market ever

62295198
>>62295173
Yeah, it's the simple saying of "Time in the market beats timing the market".
I always knew I'd be investing from the time I actually earned enough money (which wasn't until I turned 29) until I either get enough for a nice House or I turn 45. Neither has happened yet, but I sleep like a baby while watching the money grow.

62295203
>>62294870
because normalfags don't care about the mathematical trick of turning 10k into a million in 60 years

62295207
>>62294889
normalfags only talk about pussy and cock
you never touch grass

62295211
>>62295198
>Time in the market beats timing the market
meaningless bullshit unless you keep it there at least 20 years
even a 10 year period isn't enough for your meme to protect you against crashes like 2000 and 2008

62295212
>>62294870
i dont talk to people about investing. everyone knows about the stock market. some choose to participate... some choose not to.

they thought about it and dont want to invest in stocks.

62295213
>>62294980
You BTFO'd that samefagging tranny. Good job, anon. Bravo.

62295216
>>62294870
you'll understand when the stock market has a real crash, there's a reason there's lots of laws to protect pleb investors. How does losing 80% of your money and then crabbing for 30 years sound?

62295221
>>62294870
>>62294872

For a lot of people it's a mindset that many never actually obtain. Many people have a high time preference thay revolves around instant gratification. They get their monthly pay and spaff it on frivolities and consumables like drugs, alcohol or funko pop tier BS. People with low time preference understand the need for investments and savings knowing that they will benefit down the line from dividends or having a back up fund when the income dries up.
Some people learn the hard way but many simply don't learn, particularly those in welfare states.

62295226
>>62295173
>DCA for 7 years
And wait for decades to outlast any crash that might occur which basically means you're sacrificing your youth to become a wealthy geriatric. Good idea if you want to afford those Gucci diapers which are very comfy for old scrotums.

62295261
>>62295196
Unironically yes. Good luck would have meant doing the exact same thing from 2000-2012.

62295297
>>62294870
Because their news feed is filled with doomer bears who want them to fail

62295303
>>62294870
>What the hell is this psychology?
Risk aversion, which gets worse the poorer you are.

62295361
>>62294877

62295385
When the average normie has an unexpected windfall of money, the first thing that pops into their head is "what should I spend this money on?". It's like a reflex that has been embedded into them since childhood. They've been programmed by an endless stream of marketing campaigns at every stage of their life to become the ultimate consumers.

Many people are eventually able to break out of this mindset and start to invest long term, but it's usually a process that lasts several years. One or two conversations is not enough to undo a lifetime of programming

62295393
>>62295361
*takes and puts on*

62295428
>>62295385
I remember talking to an attorney who’s firm would advise a lot of lottery winners. He said it took on average 3-5 years for the lottery winners to be able to change their mindset from "spend money as fast as I can" to "I need to protect my money". Which is why like 70% of all lottery winners declare bankruptcy within 5 years. They’re not able to change their mindset fast enough and will instinctively try to spend it all

62295437
>>62295211
>bro, you need to hold AT LEAST for 40 years!
Bitchass mentality right there.
Do you even invest, or are you just trading?

62295472
>>62295428
Also every single person they've ever met will try to talk them into giving them some. They'll give in to their parents or kids or favorite cousin then the race is on
>but you gave some to X why aren't you sharing with meeeee why are you so meeeeean
And your parents/kids will say
>don't bogart that stash you should share your good fortune with X, Y, and Z and all their relatives as well
Which is why you should never ever let anybody know you've come into a windfall. Just move away and drop contact before enjoying your winnings.

62295483
>>62294877
Jews are somehow always right and somehow always win. Maybe they really are god's chosen, now I'm kind of bullish on them. How do I invest in Jews winning so I make bank?

62295630
>>62295483
Bot post

62295773
>>62294870
>Why do workers/poor hate investing?
are you retarded? most workers are poor, and they can't invest at all, their income is too low. plus, why would you invest $100 or w/e, if you can barely survive?

I'm not sure if people in this board are too shielded from reality or just deeply retarded

62295811
>>62294870
Poor and blue collar folks usually aren’t too good at long term thinking. I get the same thing with them. Whenever you talk about investing they’re like “what’s the point of money if you don’t spend it”, etc. I have a net worth of $1.5 mil at 32 from investing my money and they don’t get it.

62296144
>>62294870
Who cares. Don't talk about investing with other people unless they want to. Let them either not buy or be an exit liquidity target fund retard if they don't like dividends. Someone needs to be buying dogshit bonds to keep this charade going...let it be them. They increase the mean time between bailouts.

62296147
>>62294870

Real thread

>>62295491

62296194
>>62294913
Fucking lol. I've seen this anon's posts around the board too, he larps as a some sort of financial Jesus to poor people only to shit on them in the end. I bet OP has no money
